de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy

Class BreakpointStopCondition

  • java.lang.Object
    • de.uka.ilkd.key.symbolic_execution.strategy.BreakpointStopCondition
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition, IBreakpointStopCondition

    public class BreakpointStopCondition
extends Object
implements IBreakpointStopCondition
    An IBreakpointStopCondition which can be used during proof.
    Author:
    Martin Hentschel

    • Field Summary

      Fields 
      Modifier and Type Field and Description

      Indicates that a breakpoint is hit.

      The used IBreakpoints.

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void addBreakpoint(IBreakpoint breakpoint)
      Adds a new IBreakpoint.
      Set<IBreakpoint> getBreakpoints()
      Returns all available IBreakpoints.
      String getGoalNotAllowedMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, IGoalChooser goalChooser, long startTime, int countApplied, Goal goal)
      Returns the reason why the previous check via #isGoalAllowed(ApplyStrategy, int, long, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, Goal) has stopped the apply strategy.
      int getMaximalWork(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, IGoalChooser goalChooser)
      Returns the maximal amount of work needed to complete the task, used to display a progress bar.
      String getStopMessage(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, IGoalChooser goalChooser, long startTime, int countApplied, ApplyStrategy.SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
      Returns a human readable message which explains why the previous #shouldStop(ApplyStrategy, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
      protected boolean isBreakpointHit(SourceElement activeStatement, RuleApp ruleApp, Proof proof, Node node)
      Checks if a breakpoint is hit.
      boolean isGoalAllowed(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, IGoalChooser goalChooser, long startTime, int countApplied, Goal goal)
      Checks if it is allowed to apply the next rule on the selected Goal chosen by the IGoalChooser before it is applied.
      void removeBreakpoint(IBreakpoint breakpoint)
      Removes an IBreakpoint.
      boolean shouldStop(int maxApplications, long timeout, Proof proof, IGoalChooser goalChooser, long startTime, int countApplied, ApplyStrategy.SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
      Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.

    • Constructor Detail

      • BreakpointStopCondition

        public BreakpointStopCondition(IBreakpoint... breakpoints)
        Creates a new BreakpointStopCondition.
        Parameters:
        breakpoints - The IBreakpoint to use.

    • Method Detail

      • getMaximalWork

        public int getMaximalWork(int maxApplications,
                 long timeout,
                 Proof proof,
                 IGoalChooser goalChooser)
        Returns the maximal amount of work needed to complete the task, used to display a progress bar. Pass 0 to indicate unknown size.
        Specified by:
        getMaximalWork in interface ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition
        Parameters:
        maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        Returns:
        The maximal amount of work or 0 if it is unknown.

      • isGoalAllowed

        public boolean isGoalAllowed(int maxApplications,
                    long timeout,
                    Proof proof,
                    IGoalChooser goalChooser,
                    long startTime,
                    int countApplied,
                    Goal goal)
        Checks if it is allowed to apply the next rule on the selected Goal chosen by the IGoalChooser before it is applied. If it is not allowed the apply strategy will stop.
        Specified by:
        isGoalAllowed in interface ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition
        Parameters:
        maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
        countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
        goal - The current Goal on which the next rule will be applied.
        Returns:
        true rule application is allowed, false rule application is not allowed so stop apply strategy

      • getGoalNotAllowedMessage

        public String getGoalNotAllowedMessage(int maxApplications,
                              long timeout,
                              Proof proof,
                              IGoalChooser goalChooser,
                              long startTime,
                              int countApplied,
                              Goal goal)
        Returns the reason why the previous check via #isGoalAllowed(ApplyStrategy, int, long, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, Goal) has stopped the apply strategy.
        Specified by:
        getGoalNotAllowedMessage in interface ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition
        Parameters:
        maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
        countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
        goal - The current Goal on which the next rule will be applied.
        Returns:

      • shouldStop

        public boolean shouldStop(int maxApplications,
                 long timeout,
                 Proof proof,
                 IGoalChooser goalChooser,
                 long startTime,
                 int countApplied,
                 ApplyStrategy.SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
        Checks after each applied rule if more rules should be applied or if the strategy should stop.
        Specified by:
        shouldStop in interface ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition
        Parameters:
        maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
        countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
        singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional ApplyStrategy.SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
        Returns:
        true stop strategy, false continue strategy and apply next rule.

      • isBreakpointHit

        protected boolean isBreakpointHit(SourceElement activeStatement,
                      RuleApp ruleApp,
                      Proof proof,
                      Node node)
        Checks if a breakpoint is hit.
        Parameters:
        activeStatement - the activeStatement of the node
        ruleApp - the applied RuleApp
        proof - the current proof
        node - the current node
        Returns:
        true at least one breakpoint is hit, false all breakpoints are not hit.

      • getStopMessage

        public String getStopMessage(int maxApplications,
                    long timeout,
                    Proof proof,
                    IGoalChooser goalChooser,
                    long startTime,
                    int countApplied,
                    ApplyStrategy.SingleRuleApplicationInfo singleRuleApplicationInfo)
        Returns a human readable message which explains why the previous #shouldStop(ApplyStrategy, Proof, IGoalChooser, long, int, SingleRuleApplicationInfo) has stopped the strategy.
        Specified by:
        getStopMessage in interface ApplyStrategy.IStopCondition
        Parameters:
        maxApplications - The defined maximal number of rules to apply. Can be different to StrategySettings.getMaxSteps() in side proofs.
        timeout - The defined timeout in ms or -1 if disabled. Can be different to StrategySettings.getTimeout() in side proofs.
        proof - The current Proof.
        goalChooser - The current IGoalChooser.
        startTime - The timestamp when the apply strategy has started, computed via System.currentTimeMillis()
        countApplied - The number of already applied rules.
        singleRuleApplicationInfo - An optional ApplyStrategy.SingleRuleApplicationInfo.
        Returns:
        The human readable message which explains the stop reason.

      • addBreakpoint

        public void addBreakpoint(IBreakpoint breakpoint)
        Adds a new IBreakpoint.
        Specified by:
        addBreakpoint in interface IBreakpointStopCondition
        Parameters:
        breakpoint - The IBreakpoint to add.

      • removeBreakpoint

        public void removeBreakpoint(IBreakpoint breakpoint)
        Removes an IBreakpoint.
        Specified by:
        removeBreakpoint in interface IBreakpointStopCondition
        Parameters:
        breakpoint - The IBreakpoint to remove.

      • getBreakpoints

        public Set<IBreakpoint> getBreakpoints()
        Returns all available IBreakpoints.
        Specified by:
        getBreakpoints in interface IBreakpointStopCondition
        Returns:
        The available IBreakpoints.
